flashcards.back: The ability of a computing system to handle growing amounts of work efficiently or to be enlarged to accommodate growth.

flashcards.front: Difference between vertical and horizontal scaling
flashcards.back: Vertical scaling adds resources to a single node; horizontal scaling adds more nodes to a system.

flashcards.front: What are the five essential characteristics of cloud computing according to NIST?
flashcards.back: On-demand self-service, broad network access, resource pooling, rapid elasticity, measured service.

flashcards.front: What is the role of a load balancer in scalable systems?
flashcards.back: Distributes network traffic across multiple servers to ensure no single server becomes overwhelmed.

flashcards.front: What is the main challenge in maintaining data consistency in distributed systems?
flashcards.back: Ensuring all nodes have the same updated data despite network delays and partitions.
